commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Dashboard: only show active agents, auto-remove completed

Completed agents are automatically removed from the dashboard.
Completed agents remain accessible via the agent browser (b key).
If no agents are active, the dashboard sits idle until one spawns.

commit.message:
Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

commit.message:
Fix npm publish CI workflow and add GitHub Actions best practices (#2)

Fixes a critical bug where version bumps on main triggered npm publish twice:
the branch-triggered run published successfully, then the auto-created tag
re-triggered the workflow, causing npm publish to fail with 403 (duplicate
version). Root cause: the workflow triggered on both 'push to main' AND
'tags', causing self-inflicted re-triggers.

Changes made:
- Remove `tags: ['v*']` trigger; workflow now only fires on main branch
  pushes with version changes
- Simplify version check step (remove dead tag-triggered code path)
- Fix script injection in tag creation: use env var instead of direct
  ${{ }} interpolation in run block
- Add GitHub Actions conventions section to CLAUDE.md with lessons on
  avoiding self-trigger loops, script injection patterns, permission
  scoping, and explicit permission declarations

Also included from the initial branch:
- ESLint v9 flat config migration (eslint.config.js)
- Add globals dev dependency for ESLint v9
- Update .npmignore for flat config
- Remove unused allowedCount variable in ai-crawler-access.js
- Add ClawHub skills publishing workflow
- Add project roadmap for v1.1-v1.3 releases

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: embedded detection must run before hostname check

Lovable redirects size-whisperer-app.lovable.app → altaanaessential.io,
so the extension iframe lands on altaanaessential.io. The previous code
blocked embedded detection when hostname matched the landing set, causing
the iframe to show the landing page instead of ExtensionPanel.

Now: embedded params/iframe check runs first, hostname routing only
applies to non-embedded visits.

Update beta user count to 20 on brief page

Fix grading: increase proxy body limit to 5MB, reduce batch size to 3

- express.json() defaulted to 100KB which truncated grading prompts
- Reduced batch from 10 to 3 conversations per LLM call for smaller payloads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent iframe body scroll/overscroll in embedded mode

Adds overflow:hidden + overscroll-behavior:none to body.embedded so
the iframe has no internal scroll context. All scrolling belongs to
the parent Webflow page. Stops rubber-band/momentum glitches that
caused a gallery slice to appear above the Webflow nav on mobile
and sub-desktop viewports when the nav bar changes state.

commit.message:
Add MCP server with OAuth 2.1 and Docker deployment

Expose HelloWorld runtime over MCP (streamable HTTP) so agents without
a local Python environment can dispatch source, query vocabularies, and
send messages. OAuth 2.1 with dynamic client registration, PKCE S256,
and SQLite-backed token persistence for container restarts.

New files: src/mcp_server.py, src/mcp_auth.py, Dockerfile, .dockerignore
Tests: 38 new tests (both memory and SQLite backends)

adding a 2-second delay, blocking the auto clicker. Now fastForwardWave

every 100ms, skipping 1000 waves each tick.

commit.message:
Add auction data import and pot-based survival curves (#27)

* Add historical auction import and pot-based survival curves

- Parse 351 historical auction records from Excel spreadsheet (2023-2026)
- Add bulk JSON import with deduplication (by ID and content matching)
- Holiday tier classification: Major (Christmas, Thanksgiving, July 4th,
  New Year's), Standard (Memorial Day, Labor Day), Minor (MLK, Presidents)
- Pot-based survival model: P(available day N) = P(historicalPot > dailyRate×N)
  Uses final pot amounts as proxy for time-to-claim, works without daysOpen data
- Position-aware filtering: 4th call shows faster claiming than 1st call
- EV optimizer now has tier and position filter dropdowns with model info display
- Import JSON button in History tab with file upload handler
- Record count summary with year breakdown in auction table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add package/single-day distinction and auto-import historical data

- Embed 351 historical auction records directly in app for auto-import
- autoImportHistoricalData() runs on admin login, loads into Firestore
- Add shiftDays field: 3 for Fri-Sun packages, 1 for individual shifts
- Add positions array: captures all rotation positions (e.g. [1st,4th,2nd])
- Package vs single-day filter in EV optimizer prevents apples-to-oranges
  comparisons: package pots ($1K-$3.8K for 3 days) separated from
  single-day pots ($100-$4.6K for 1 day)
- Position filter now checks positions array, so filtering by "4th call"
  includes A (1,4,2) packages where 4th appears on any day
- Fix deduplication to include notes field, distinguishing D from E
  rotations that share the same position and pot amount
- Verified: 4th call survival 57% at day 5 vs 1st call 87% — confirms
  4th gets claimed faster, optimal day 4 vs day 8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(scraper): auto-rename misnamed EasyEcom inventory file before DB populate

EasyEcom sometimes returns a CSV file with a .zip extension. This caused
two failures: (1) _store_wh_stock_to_db skipped parsing due to wrong suffix,
and (2) the populate step's file glob (**/*.csv, **/*.xlsx) couldn't find the
file, resulting in 'No files found' and zero rows imported.

Fix: detect real format from magic bytes in _extract_from_zip and rename
to .csv or .xlsx before returning, so both the in-scraper upsert and the
workflow's Populate DB fallback step always find the correct file.

fix: types/index.ts 누락 타입 복원 - feat/tracking 병합으로 사라진 ColorTheme, RoutineEvaluation, KPT 필드 등 복구

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Firebase Admin SDK with REST API for user management

Firebase Admin SDK requires a service account private key which is
blocked by the org's security policy. Switched to Firebase Auth REST
API (signUp endpoint) for creating users. Delete now only removes
from our DB since the REST API requires the user's own token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add OCaml, Julia, and Gleam language support (34 languages) (#530)

OCaml (.ml, .mli): let bindings, type definitions (variant/record/alias),
modules, function application tracking via value_path.

Julia (.jl): functions, structs, abstract types, modules, macros.
Name extraction from signature → call_expression → identifier.

Gleam (.gleam): functions, type definitions, type aliases, constants.
Qualified calls via field_access, direct calls via function_call.

Each language includes: LanguageDef with chunk/call queries, post-process
classification, return type extraction, unit tests (18 total), integration
tests (3), fixtures, and full mod.rs/eval_common wiring.

commit.message:
Fix admin dashboard: use anon key fallback so SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is not required

Supabase RLS allows anon key SELECT on candidate_results, so hardcode the
anon key as a fallback in get-results.js. Removes the 503 'Service key not
configured' error from the admin dashboard without needing any env var changes.
